questions.question: Why use a Node version manager?
options: To keep projects on the runtime versions they expect
questions.explanation: Version managers let you isolate runtime versions by project.

questions.question: What is a common advantage of Bun in frontend work?
options: It can offer fast runtime and tooling workflows
It replaces the need for TypeScript
questions.explanation: Bun is often used for fast execution and built-in tooling, though Node remains the most common baseline.

questions.question: What should you check before debugging a dependency issue?
questions.explanation: Many install and runtime problems come from the wrong Node version being active.

questions.question: Why document the expected runtime in the project?
options: So the repo can be opened without any build tools
So teammates and CI use the same version
questions.explanation: Documenting the runtime reduces surprises for teammates and automation.
